title: Implement HTTP API for EventMesh
description: Build RESTful HTTP API with JWT authentication, event publishing/subscribing, and Server-Sent Events streaming. Design documented in docs/EventMesh-HTTP-API-Implementation-Plan.md

title: HTTP API Implementation Epic
description: Complete RESTful HTTP API with JWT authentication, event publishing/subscribing, and Server-Sent Events streaming for EventMesh. Replaces eventmesh-go-1.

title: Remove redundant docs index
description: Remove docs/README.md as a redundant index, move useful documentation maintenance rules into AGENTS.md, and update references so the root README remains the docs entry point.
close_reason: Removed redundant docs/README.md, moved documentation maintenance rules into AGENTS.md, updated root docs map, and verified no Markdown links are missing.

title: Agent-friendly EventMesh north star
description: Track the product direction for making EventMesh easy for humans, services, and AI agents to inspect, understand, replay, validate, publish into, and debug safely. This is positioning and roadmap work, not a claim about current implementation.
acceptance_criteria: North-star documentation exists, and concrete child issues cover event metadata, discovery, safe publishing, replay/context, MCP tooling, and explainable API errors.

title: Define agent-friendly event envelope metadata
description: Design the minimum event metadata envelope that makes events self-describing without making normal publishing awkward. Consider event ID, topic, type, source, timestamp, schema reference, correlation ID, trace ID, causation ID, and optional summary fields.
acceptance_criteria: A design note or issue comment specifies the MVP event envelope, backwards-compatibility approach, validation expectations, and test cases needed before implementation.

title: Design topic/schema/capability discovery API
description: Design HTTP and CLI discovery surfaces that let clients and agents list topics, inspect topic metadata, find schema references, discover publishers/subscribers where safe, and understand supported EventMesh capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: Discovery API proposal identifies endpoints, response shapes, auth visibility rules, CLI affordances, and the first implementation slice with tests.

title: Add draft and validate flow for safe publishing
description: Explore a safe publishing flow where agents and clients can draft or validate events before publishing. Validation should check topic rules, auth, required metadata, schema expectations, and payload shape without appending to the EventLog.
acceptance_criteria: Design and tests cover a non-mutating validation path, structured validation errors, and how successful validation relates to the existing POST /api/v1/events publish path.

title: Design replay search and timeline views for agent context
description: Design replay/context APIs that help humans and agents reconstruct what happened by topic, client, source, correlation ID, trace ID, and time window. Keep current in-memory limits explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Proposal defines the first replay/search/timeline endpoint or CLI command, response shape, filtering semantics, current storage limitations, and tests for at least one context-recovery workflow.

title: Create EventMesh MCP server design
description: Design an MCP server that exposes EventMesh operations as tool calls for agents. Candidate tools include list_topics, describe_topic, replay_events, validate_event, publish_event, list_subscriptions, and trace_correlation.
acceptance_criteria: Design identifies the initial MCP tool set, auth/config model, mapping to existing HTTP/client APIs, safety constraints, and a small first implementation milestone.

title: Standardize explainable API error responses
description: Make API failures structured and specific enough for clients and agents to self-correct. Errors should include stable codes, human-readable messages, relevant field paths, and safe suggestions where useful.
acceptance_criteria: A documented error response contract exists, at least one endpoint has tests for stable error codes and field-level details, and docs explain how clients should react.

title: Make peer subscription routing wildcard-aware
description: Control-plane subscription gossip currently records remote peer interests by exact topic key. Add failing tests showing a remote peer subscribed to patterns like orders.* receives data-plane events for orders.created, then implement wildcard-aware interested-peer matching consistent with the RoutingTable semantics.

title: Implement control-plane heartbeat lifecycle
description: ControlPlanePeerLink exposes SendHeartbeat, StartHeartbeats, and StopHeartbeats, but StartHeartbeats/StopHeartbeats are not implemented and received heartbeat frames are not handled. Add tests for heartbeat send/receive/health transitions, then implement the lifecycle without mixing heartbeat behavior into the data-plane event path.

title: Give control-plane messages independent queueing or priority
description: Data-plane and control-plane messages are typed separately but currently share one per-peer send queue. Add tests that control messages are not starved behind data-plane traffic, then introduce either separate queues or priority selection while preserving the public PeerLink interfaces.

title: Narrow MeshNode dependencies to focused peerlink interfaces
description: GRPCMeshNode currently stores peerlink.CompletePeerLink, so the data/control/connection split is not enforced at the MeshNode boundary. Add tests or compile-time checks around the methods each MeshNode path needs, then split fields or constructor dependencies into DataPlanePeerLink, ControlPlanePeerLink, and PeerConnectionManager where practical.

title: Make mesh networking tests assert behavior instead of diagnostics
description: Some multi-node/PeerLink integration tests log that gossip or delivery may need refinement instead of failing. After the underlying behavior is fixed, convert those diagnostic sections into strict assertions for subscription gossip, interested-peer routing, and data-plane delivery.

title: Clean stale data/control-plane comments
description: Several comments still mention old phase notes or special subscription events even though subscription gossip now uses control-plane messages. Clean these comments after behavioral fixes so future agents do not follow outdated implementation hints.

title: Make TDD guidance explicit
description: Clarify in AGENTS.md and README.md that behavior changes should start with failing tests, then pass and refactor, so humans and future agents treat TDD as a core codebase tenet.
close_reason: Made TDD guidance explicit in AGENTS.md and README.md; verified Markdown links including the current docs map.

title: Create internal/httpapi package structure
description: Set up basic package structure for HTTP API: server.go, handlers.go, auth.go, types.go, middleware.go as outlined in design doc

title: [Task 100] Create internal/httpapi package structure
description: Epic 10, Task 100: Set up basic package structure for HTTP API: server.go, handlers.go, auth.go, types.go, middleware.go

title: [Task 101] Implement JWT authentication middleware
description: Epic 10, Task 101: Create JWT-based authentication for client isolation. Simple clientId-based tokens for MVP

title: [Task 102] Create event publishing endpoints
description: Epic 10, Task 102: Implement POST /api/v1/events endpoint that integrates with existing MeshNode.PublishEvent method

title: [Task 103] Implement Server-Sent Events streaming
description: Epic 10, Task 103: Create GET /api/v1/events/stream endpoint for real-time event delivery using SSE and existing subscription system

title: [Task 104] Create subscription management endpoints
description: Epic 10, Task 104: Implement POST/GET/DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions endpoints for client subscription management with proper client isolation

title: [Task 105] Implement admin debugging endpoints
description: Epic 10, Task 105: Create GET /api/v1/admin/* endpoints for system-wide monitoring: clients, subscriptions, stats, health

title: [Task 106] Add HTTP server integration to main.go
description: Epic 10, Task 106: Add --http and --http-port flags to cmd/eventmesh/main.go to start HTTP API server alongside gRPC server

title: [Epic 90] Code Quality & Maintenance
description: Epic for code quality improvements, linting fixes, and maintenance tasks

title: Create internal/httpapi package structure
description: Set up basic package structure for HTTP API: server.go, handlers.go, auth.go, types.go, middleware.go as outlined in design doc

title: [Task 900] Fix remaining linting issues
description: Epic 90, Task 900: Address remaining 43 linting issues: 39 errcheck (unchecked Close() calls), 4 staticcheck (empty branches, unused results)
close_reason: Superseded by newer lint-tracking issue eventmesh-go-73, which now includes current triage notes and dependency on the Go toolchain setup.

title: [Task 203] Server-Sent Events (SSE) Client
close_reason: Implemented: pkg/httpclient has StreamClient/SSE processing and tests. Remaining reconnect/resume behavior is tracked separately by eventmesh-go-59.

title: [Task 204] Publisher/Subscriber Example Applications
close_reason: Implemented: examples/ contains single-node, multi-node, and CLI usage publisher/subscriber scripts. Follow-up simplification remains tracked under the examples audit epic.

title: [Task 206] Multi-Node Server Orchestration
close_reason: Implemented enough for current stage: examples/multi-node includes orchestration scripts and server startup helpers. Docker/production orchestration remains tracked separately.

title: [Task 207] Cross-Node Event Flow Testing
close_reason: Implemented: internal/meshnode/node_integration_test.go includes real multi-node networking, subscription gossip, and intelligent routing tests.

title: Implement JWT authentication middleware
description: Create JWT-based authentication for client isolation. Simple clientId-based tokens for MVP as per design

title: Add production metrics endpoint and health monitoring
description: Health and admin stats endpoints exist, but there is no production metrics endpoint or comprehensive observability surface yet. Add metrics for events, subscriptions, peers, queues, drops, errors, and latency once the core API defects are fixed.

title: Replace inmemory event log with RocksDB
Comprehensive Documentation Improvements
description: Refresh repository documentation so README, design notes, examples, and agent guidance match the current EventMesh codebase, APIs, build/test loop, and subscription/SSE behavior.
close_reason: Refreshed repository docs around current MVP behavior, API contracts, examples, discovery notes, benchmark guidance, and agent workflow; removed stale scratch/design docs after local validation.

title: Implement mTLS security for peer-to-peer communication
description:
## Overview
Implement mutual TLS (mTLS) security for secure communication between EventMesh nodes as outlined in design.md Appendix B.

## Current Status
- mTLS is documented in design.md but marked as 'deferred from MVP'
- PeerLink interfaces reference mTLS but current implementation lacks security
- No TLS/crypto code exists in codebase currently

## Requirements (from design.md)
- **CA Setup**: Use smallstep step-ca as internal Certificate Authority  
- **Node Enrollment**: Each node generates keypair on startup, gets cert from step-ca
- **mTLS Configuration**: Services use tls.RequireAndVerifyClientCert
- **Rotation & Revocation**: Short-lived certs (24h) with automatic renewal
- **Node Identity**: Certificate SANs encode node identities (e.g., spiffe://mesh/prod/node-42)

## Implementation Tasks
1. Set up step-ca integration for certificate management
2. Add TLS configuration to PeerLink implementations  
3. Implement certificate rotation and renewal
4. Add node identity verification
5. Update PeerLink to use secure gRPC with mTLS
6. Add security tests and validation

## Priority
P2 - Important for production deployment but not blocking MVP functionality

title: SSE + Subscription API integration strategy
description:
## Overview
Currently we have two separate subscription mechanisms that work independently. Need to evaluate and potentially unify the approach.

## Current State
**SSE Stream Subscriptions:**
- Subscribe via GET /api/v1/events/stream?topic=pattern
- Creates temporary HTTPClient subscriptions for real-time delivery
- Subscriptions disappear on disconnect

**Subscription Management API:**
- POST /api/v1/subscriptions with {"topic": "pattern"} 
- GET /api/v1/subscriptions to list persistent subscriptions
- DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id} to remove specific subscriptions
- Stored persistently in MeshNode

## Questions to Resolve
1. Should SSE subscriptions appear in GET /api/v1/subscriptions response?
2. Should POST /api/v1/subscriptions automatically enable SSE delivery?
3. Should we maintain separate temporary vs persistent subscription models?
4. How should subscription persistence work across SSE reconnections?

## Implementation Considerations
- May need to distinguish between 'streaming' and 'persistent' subscription types
- Consider adding subscription metadata (type, created_via, etc.)
- Need to maintain backward compatibility during any changes
- Update design documentation with chosen approach

## Priority
P1 - Important architectural decision that affects API design and user experience
close_reason: Superseded by concrete architecture task eventmesh-go-58 and newly-created bug eventmesh-go-92 covering the current SSE topic-query mismatch.

title: Topic filtering interface consistency
description:
## Overview
Currently we have different interfaces for specifying topics across our APIs, which may cause confusion for API consumers.

## Current Inconsistency
**SSE Stream Endpoint:**
- Uses query parameter: GET /api/v1/events/stream?topic=pattern
- Simple string parameter

**Subscription Management API:**
- Uses JSON body: POST /api/v1/subscriptions with {"topic": "pattern"}
- Structured JSON request

## Impact
- Different patterns for the same concept (topic specification)
- Potential confusion for API consumers
- Inconsistent documentation and examples

## Possible Solutions
1. **Standardize on Query Parameters**: Change POST to use ?topic= parameter
2. **Standardize on JSON Bodies**: Change SSE to accept POST with JSON body
3. **Support Both**: Allow both formats on all endpoints for flexibility
4. **Document Rationale**: Keep current design but clearly document why each uses different approach

## Implementation Considerations
- SSE traditionally uses GET requests (RESTful for read-only streaming)
- Subscription creation naturally fits POST with JSON body
- Query parameters are simpler for streaming use cases
- JSON bodies allow for more complex subscription metadata

## Recommendation
Likely keep current design but improve documentation to explain the rationale for each approach.

## Priority
P2 - API consistency improvement, but not blocking functionality

title: HTTPClient local subscription tracking cleanup
description:
## Overview
After refactoring subscription management to use MeshNode as single source of truth, HTTPClient still maintains local subscription tracking that may no longer be necessary.

## Current State
**HTTPClient has local subscription tracking:**
- AddSubscription(), GetSubscriptions(), RemoveSubscription() methods
- Local subscriptions map with mutex protection
- Originally designed for per-client state management

**MeshNode now handles subscription state:**
- GetClientSubscriptions() provides authoritative subscription data
- UnsubscribeByID() manages subscription lifecycle
- All HTTP handlers delegate to MeshNode methods

## Analysis Needed
1. **Still Used?** Audit codebase to see if HTTPClient local tracking is still used
2. **SSE Integration** Check if SSE streaming still relies on HTTPClient.DeliverEvent()
3. **Performance** Evaluate if local caching provides performance benefits

## Possible Actions
1. **Remove Unused Code**: If local tracking isn't used, remove it entirely
2. **Clarify Purpose**: If still needed, document why both local and MeshNode tracking exist
3. **Hybrid Approach**: Use MeshNode for persistence, HTTPClient for active streaming state

## Investigation Tasks
- [ ] Grep codebase for HTTPClient.AddSubscription usage
- [ ] Check if DeliverEvent() method relies on local subscription state
- [ ] Review SSE streaming implementation dependencies
- [ ] Test removing local tracking to see what breaks

## Priority
P2 - Code cleanup and architecture clarification, but not urgent

title: Add comprehensive subscription + SSE integration test
description:
## Overview
Create an integration test that validates the complete flow of using both Subscription Management API and SSE streaming together.

## Current Testing Gap
**Existing Tests:**
- Subscription API: POST, GET, DELETE tested independently
- SSE streaming: end-to-end event delivery tested independently 
- **Missing**: Tests that combine both APIs in realistic workflows

## Test Scenarios Needed
1. **Subscription → SSE Flow**:
   - Create subscription via POST /api/v1/subscriptions
   - Connect to SSE stream /api/v1/events/stream  
   - Publish event and verify it's delivered via SSE
   - Verify subscription appears in GET /api/v1/subscriptions

2. **SSE → Subscription Management**:
   - Connect to SSE with topic filter
   - Check if subscription appears in API (depending on architecture decision)
   - Delete subscription and verify SSE behavior

3. **Multi-Client Integration**:
   - Multiple clients with different subscriptions
   - Verify proper client isolation
   - Event delivery to correct clients only

## Implementation Details
- Combine existing test patterns from TestCreateSubscription and TestStreamEvents
- Test both authenticated and error scenarios
- Verify subscription metadata consistency
- Test connection lifecycle (connect → subscribe → events → disconnect → reconnect)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Complete workflow test passes
- [ ] Proper error handling for edge cases
- [ ] Client isolation verified
- [ ] Subscription state consistency validated

## Priority
P1 - Important for validating the integration architecture works as expected

title: Improve error handling with typed errors
description:
## Overview
Replace string-based error matching with proper error types for more robust error handling and classification.

## Current Approach
String matching in DeleteSubscription handler for error classification.

## Issues with Current Approach
- Fragile: Changes to error messages break error detection
- Hard to test: Need to match exact error text
- Not maintainable: Error conditions scattered across string checks
- Internationalization: Cannot localize error messages easily

## Proposed Solution
Define proper error types with constants for subscription errors, update MeshNode to return typed errors, and update HTTP handlers to use error type checking instead of string matching.

## Implementation Plan
1. Define error types in pkg/meshnode or internal/errors
2. Update MeshNode methods to return typed errors
3. Update HTTP handlers to use error type checking
4. Add comprehensive error handling tests
5. Update documentation with error specifications

## Benefits
- More robust error handling
- Better testability
- Clearer API contracts
- Easier maintenance
- Foundation for API documentation

## Priority
P3 - Quality improvement, but current string matching works for MVP

title: Create event publishing endpoints
description: Implement POST /api/v1/events endpoint that integrates with existing MeshNode.PublishEvent method

title: Add subscription metrics and observability
description:
## Overview
Add metrics and observability for subscription management to support monitoring and debugging in production environments.

## Current State
No metrics are currently collected for subscription operations, making it difficult to monitor system health and usage patterns.

## Proposed Metrics
**Subscription Counters:**
- Total active subscriptions per client
- Subscription creation rate
- Subscription deletion rate
- Failed subscription attempts

**SSE Stream Metrics:**
- Active SSE connections count
- SSE connection duration
- Events delivered per subscription
- SSE connection failures

**Performance Metrics:**
- Subscription operation latency
- MeshNode subscription lookup time
- Event delivery latency

## Implementation Approach
1. Add metrics collection to HTTP handlers
2. Add metrics to MeshNode subscription operations
3. Expose metrics via /metrics endpoint (Prometheus format)
4. Add health check indicators for subscription system
5. Consider structured logging for subscription events

## Benefits
- Production monitoring capabilities
- Performance bottleneck identification
- Usage pattern analysis
- Debugging support
- SLA/SLO measurement foundation

## Integration Points
- HTTP handlers (operation metrics)
- MeshNode (state metrics)
- SSE streaming (connection metrics)
- Health endpoint (system health)

## Priority
P3 - Important for production but not blocking MVP functionality

title: Add shell completion setup instructions to examples documentation
description: Add instructions for setting up bash/zsh/fish autocompletion for eventmesh-cli in the examples documentation. This will improve user experience by enabling tab completion for commands, flags, and options.

title: Epic: Add Offset-Based Event Replay
description: Expose EventMesh's existing offset-based replay capabilities through HTTP API and CLI. The EventLog core already supports ReadFromTopic() and ReplayTopic() with offset parameters, but these aren't accessible via user-facing interfaces. This epic will add HTTP endpoints and CLI commands for historical event access, enabling checkpoint-based processing, debugging, and data recovery workflows.

title: Add HTTP API endpoints for offset-based event reading
description: Implement new HTTP endpoints: GET /api/v1/events/{topic}?offset={start}&limit={count} for batch reading, GET /api/v1/events/{topic}/replay?offset={start} for streaming replay, and GET /api/v1/events/{topic}/info for topic metadata. Wire these to existing EventLog.ReadFromTopic() and ReplayTopic() methods.

title: Add CLI replay command with offset support
description: Add new 'eventmesh-cli replay' command with --topic, --offset, and --limit parameters. Extend existing 'stream' command with --offset parameter. Add 'topics info' command to show topic metadata (current offset, total events).

title: Update examples and documentation for offset-based replay
description: Add examples demonstrating offset-based replay for checkpoint-based processing, debugging workflows, and data recovery. Update API documentation and add use case documentation showing practical applications.

title: Examples Audit & Cleanup Epic
description: Clean up and simplify examples directory to focus on essential functionality during active development phase. Remove overly complex examples and ensure consistency with current HTTP API. Priority: reduce complexity while maintaining clear learning progression.

title: Task 461: Audit Current Examples
description: Review each example directory for API compatibility and assess complexity vs. development stage appropriateness. Document which examples are essential vs. complex demos that should be simplified or removed during active development.

title: Task 462: Review examples/basic for Relevance
description: Check if examples/basic/main.go is still relevant since it uses internal EventLog APIs directly (bypassing HTTP API layer). Determine if it should be updated to use HTTP API, moved to a different location, or removed entirely to maintain consistency with current architecture.

title: Task 463: Simplify/Remove Complex Examples
description: Evaluate complex examples (cli-usage/order-workflow.sh 245 lines, pattern-subscriber.sh 131 lines, single-node/demo.sh 139 lines) and determine if they should be simplified or removed during active development phase. Keep focus on core functionality demonstrations rather than complex business workflows.

title: Implement Server-Sent Events streaming
description: Create GET /api/v1/events/stream endpoint for real-time event delivery using SSE and existing subscription system

title: Task 464: Update README Files Consistency
description: Ensure all example README files match actual example content and current codebase functionality. Update main examples/README.md to reflect any changes from cleanup. Verify all scripts and instructions work with current EventMesh HTTP API and CLI commands.

title: Task 465: Consolidate Essential Examples
description: Keep only examples that demonstrate core EventMesh functionality clearly. Ensure each remaining example serves a distinct educational purpose and creates a clear learning progression from simple pub/sub to more advanced features. Remove redundant or overlapping examples.

title: Comprehensive CLI replay functionality verification
description: Verify all CLI replay commands work correctly including replay, topics info, and stream --offset functionality. Ensure edge cases are handled and error messages are clear.

title: Add Docker Compose multi-node EventMesh setup
description: Create docker-compose.yml with 2-3 EventMesh nodes for distributed testing. Include service discovery, proper port mapping, environment variables for node configuration, documentation in examples/docker/ directory, --no-auth mode for development, and scaling instructions.

title: Create parameterized start-meshnode.sh script
description:
Create a new parameterized script 'start-meshnode.sh' that accepts a node-id parameter for starting EventMesh nodes with unique identifiers.

## Requirements:
- Create examples/simple/start-meshnode.sh script
- Accept node-id as command-line parameter (e.g., ./start-meshnode.sh node-1)
- Generate unique configurations per node (ports, data directories, etc.)
- Provide validation and error handling for missing/invalid node-id
- Keep existing start-server.sh unchanged for single-node use
- Include usage examples in script comments

## Success Criteria:
- Script can start multiple EventMesh instances simultaneously
- Each instance has unique node-id and configuration
- No port conflicts between nodes
- Clear usage documentation and examples
- Integration with existing simple/ example structure

title: Implement mesh node discovery mechanism
description:
Design and implement a discovery mechanism for new EventMesh nodes to find and connect to existing nodes in the mesh network.

## Problem Statement:
When a new EventMesh node starts up, it needs a way to discover other active nodes in the mesh to establish peer connections and participate in the distributed event streaming network.

## Research Areas:
- Static configuration (seed nodes, bootstrap lists)
- Dynamic discovery (DNS-based, multicast, service discovery systems)
- Hybrid approaches (static seeds + gossip protocol)
- Integration with existing PeerLink architecture

## Requirements:
- New nodes can discover existing mesh nodes automatically
- Resilient to node failures and network partitions
- Configurable discovery mechanism (support multiple strategies)
- Integration with current MeshNode and PeerLink components
- Consider security implications of node discovery
- Support both development and production scenarios

## Success Criteria:
- New node can join an existing mesh without manual configuration
- Discovery mechanism is fault-tolerant
- Clear configuration options for different deployment scenarios
- Documentation for setup and troubleshooting
- Integration tests demonstrating multi-node mesh formation

## Implementation Considerations:
- Review existing PeerLink implementation for compatibility
- Consider discovery service dependencies and complexity
- Plan for bootstrap/chicken-and-egg scenarios
- Design for eventual consistency in mesh topology

title: Replace --http-secret flag with environment variable
description: Replace insecure command-line JWT secret flag with EVENTMESH_JWT_SECRET environment variable. Remove --http-secret flag entirely, update all examples and tests. Eliminates process list secret exposure and aligns with security best practices.

title: Implement proper secret management system
description: Future work: Implement production-grade secret management with configuration file support, external secret providers (Vault, Kubernetes Secrets, AWS Secrets Manager), secret rotation capabilities, and SecretProvider interface architecture.

title: Implement unified subscription architecture
description: Replace dual subscription model with persistent subscriptions + SSE streaming. Remove temporary SSE subscriptions (?topic= parameter), make SSE stream ALL active subscriptions for client, use persistent subscription API as single source of truth. Clean up HTTPClient local subscription tracking. This aligns with industry standards like Kafka, Pub/Sub, and other production event streaming systems.

title: Implement SSE reconnection with event resume
description: Add production-grade reconnection support with hybrid cursor model. Phase 1: Basic resume support with ?since=eventId parameter. Phase 2: Enhanced control with force=true override. Hybrid model: max(server_cursor, client_hint) provides safety defaults with client flexibility. Handles normal reconnect, catch-up, recovery, and intentional rewind scenarios.

title: Create subscription management endpoints
description: Implement POST/GET/DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions endpoints for client subscription management with proper client isolation

title: Add reconnection safety and operational features
description: Future work for production-grade operational controls. Server cursor tracking per subscription for safety and monitoring. Retention boundary enforcement to prevent unbounded history requests. Replay rate limiting and budgets to protect against resource exhaustion. Monitoring and observability for operational visibility. Builds on hybrid reconnection model for production deployment.

title: Fix HTTP Response Header Issue
description: Fix superfluous response.WriteHeader call errors in HTTP API server. The writeJSON method in internal/httpapi/server.go calls w.WriteHeader() but some callers may have already set headers, causing log noise and potential HTTP response bugs.

title: Clean up remaining TODOs after targeted bugs are fixed
description: Broad cleanup issue for remaining TODO/FIXME comments. Several original TODOs have already been addressed or converted into specific issues. Treat this as a sweep after higher-priority defects are resolved, not as primary ready work.

title: Improve Test Coverage and Reliability
description: Improve reliability and coverage after the Go toolchain is available. Current repo has extensive tests, including HTTP API, routing, eventlog, PeerLink, and multi-node MeshNode tests, but make test could not be run in this environment because go is not on PATH. First unblock local test execution via eventmesh-go-94, then address flaky or low-coverage packages.
notes: Triage 2026-04-24: Test harness reliability blockers fixed. cmd/eventmesh no longer shells out to go run/fixed localhost port for TestHTTPIntegration. internal/meshnode TestPeerLinkToMeshNodeIntegration now uses ephemeral ports, actual PeerLink listening address, and condition-based waits. Full env GOCACHE=/tmp/eventmesh-go-build-cache GOMODCACHE=/tmp/eventmesh-go-mod-cache go test ./... -timeout 2m passes.

title: Code Quality and Refactoring
description: Refactor large files for better maintainability: internal/httpapi/handlers.go (880 lines) could benefit from splitting, internal/meshnode/node.go (894 lines) may need organization, and internal/peerlink/grpc.go (787 lines) contains complex networking code that could be modularized.

title: Documentation Completeness
description: Improve documentation across the codebase: some internal packages lack proper documentation, API documentation could be expanded, and consider adding more examples for different use cases beyond the current multi-node, single-node, and CLI usage examples.

title: Add error handling/reconnection for gRPC stream failures
description: In internal/peerlink/grpc.go:448, when gRPC stream.Send() fails, the code just returns without proper error handling or reconnection. This is critical for mesh network reliability as it can cause permanent disconnections between nodes.

title: Add logging/metrics for dropped events
description: In internal/httpapi/handlers.go:58, when the event channel is full, events are silently dropped with just a comment. Need to add slog.Warn or metrics to track when this happens for observability and debugging.

title: Implement proper event counting in EventLog
description: In internal/httpapi/handlers.go:668, admin stats endpoint shows 0 events published because EventLog doesn't track event counts. Need to add proper event counting for admin monitoring and statistics.

title: Track actual client connection times
description: In internal/httpapi/handlers.go:566, admin endpoint shows time.Now() instead of actual client connection time. Need to track real connection timestamps for better admin visibility and debugging.

title: Implement admin debugging endpoints
description: Create GET /api/v1/admin/* endpoints for system-wide monitoring: clients, subscriptions, stats, health

title: Track actual subscription creation times
description: In internal/httpapi/handlers.go:614, admin endpoint shows time.Now() instead of actual subscription creation time. Similar to connection time tracking, need real creation timestamps for admin monitoring.

title: Remove obsolete TODO comments from codebase
description: Clean up obsolete TODO comments: internal/peerlink/grpc.go:488,648 (commented logging), internal/httpapi/middleware.go:141 (structured logging comment), internal/httpapi/handlers.go:742,832 (explanatory comments), internal/peerlink/grpc_test.go:686 (obsolete test comment), internal/httpapi/events_test.go:29,39,48 (placeholder test comments). These TODOs are no longer relevant.

title: Make SSE keepalive interval configurable
description: In internal/httpapi/handlers.go:816, keepalive interval is hardcoded to 2 seconds for testing. Consider making this configurable for production deployments. Low priority as current implementation works fine.

title: Fix golangci-lint issues (56 items)
description: Address remaining golangci-lint/staticcheck/errcheck findings after the Go toolchain and linter path are usable. Keep production-code issues ahead of test cleanup. Original notes mentioned many errcheck items, mostly in tests.
notes: PROGRESS: Fixed 7 critical production code issues (gRPC/HTTP resource cleanup, dead code removal, optimizations). 52 issues remain - mostly test file errcheck items. Production code is now clean.

title: EventMesh Routing Architecture Testing Epic
description: Comprehensive testing and verification of the core EventMesh routing functionality including multi-node event flow, subscription propagation, and intelligent routing based on subscription interest. This ensures the fundamental peer-to-peer mesh architecture works as designed.
notes: Triage 2026-04-24: core routing tests are much stronger than this epic title suggests; real multi-node networking, subscription gossip, and intelligent routing tests exist. Remaining child work should focus on resilience/performance scenarios, not basic architecture proof.

title: Create real multi-node integration test with actual networking
description: Replace the current simulated multi-node test with a real end-to-end test that uses actual PeerLink networking. Test scenario: Publisher connects to Node A, Subscriber connects to Node B, verify events flow Node A → PeerLink → Node B → Local subscriber. Validate both regular events and subscription propagation events work through the mesh.

title: Implement subscription-based intelligent routing tests
description: Create tests that verify subscription propagation enables intelligent routing. Test scenario: Node A has no subscribers for topic X, Node B subscribes to topic X, verify Node B's subscription propagates to Node A. When publishing to topic X on Node A, events should route only to nodes with interested subscribers (Node B). Nodes without subscribers should not receive events.

title: Verify PeerLink ReceiveEvents channel implementation
description: Audit and test the PeerLink ReceiveEvents channel implementation to ensure it properly handles incoming events from peer nodes. Verify the channel-based event streaming works correctly, handles backpressure, and integrates properly with the MeshNode's handleIncomingPeerEvents goroutine.

title: Test subscription gossip protocol end-to-end
description: Create comprehensive tests for the subscription gossip mechanism. Verify that when nodes subscribe/unsubscribe, the subscription changes propagate correctly to all connected peers via the propagateSubscriptionChange function. Test subscription state synchronization across the mesh and validate that peer nodes maintain accurate routing tables.

title: Add network resilience test scenarios
description: Implement tests for network resilience scenarios including peer disconnection/reconnection, event queuing during network partitions, and subscription state rebuild after network healing. Verify the mesh can handle network failures gracefully and maintain event delivery guarantees.

title: Add HTTP server integration to main.go
description: Add --http and --http-port flags to cmd/eventmesh/main.go to start HTTP API server alongside gRPC server

title: PeerLink ReceiveEvents performance testing
description: Performance and throughput testing for PeerLink ReceiveEvents channel implementation. Test scenarios: high-throughput event streaming, multiple peer event sources, resource usage monitoring under load, memory leak prevention during sustained operation. This ensures the PeerLink can handle production-level event volumes.

title: Review PeerLink tests for fitness of purpose
Define PublishEvent delivery guarantees and peer failure behavior
description: PublishEvent now persists locally first and returns DeliveryResult failures for local/peer delivery errors. Remaining design work: decide whether peer forwarding failures should fail the publisher request, be queued for retry, or be reported asynchronously. Align implementation with at-least-once semantics and document behavior for partial delivery.

title: Separate Data Plane and Control Plane Communication
description: Currently, user events (data plane) and subscription gossip messages (control plane) share the same gRPC stream, causing head-of-line blocking, mixed QoS requirements, and monitoring complexity. This epic separates them into distinct communication channels with independent performance characteristics.
acceptance_criteria: Data plane and control plane use separate communication channels. No head-of-line blocking between user events and subscription updates. Independent QoS policies for each plane. Clear observability separation.
notes: Triage 2026-04-24: interface-level and protobuf-message-level separation is already partly implemented. The open architectural decision is whether to physically split gRPC streams/RPCs or keep one stream with typed frames. See eventmesh-go-85 and eventmesh-go-86.

title: Split PeerLink into focused interfaces using Interface Segregation Principle
description: Replace monolithic PeerLink interface with 4 specialized interfaces: PeerConnectionManager (connection lifecycle, handshakes), DataPlaneTransport (user event streaming), ControlPlaneTransport (subscription propagation, heartbeats), and PeerHealthMonitor (health checks, peer discovery). Update pkg/peerlink/interfaces.go with new interface definitions.
close_reason: Implemented: pkg/peerlink now defines focused DataPlanePeerLink, ControlPlanePeerLink, PeerConnectionManager, and CompletePeerLink interfaces with interface tests.

title: Decide and implement true separate PeerLink data/control RPC streams
description: Current code has data/control plane message types and focused Go interfaces, but proto/peerlink/v1/peerlink.proto still exposes one EventStream RPC carrying all PeerMessage variants. Decide whether to keep one physical stream with typed frames or split into DataStream and ControlStream RPCs. If splitting, update proto, regenerate Go bindings, and adapt GRPCPeerLink tests.

title: Refactor GRPCPeerLink if true separate streams are chosen
description: Current GRPCPeerLink implements focused SendEvent and SendSubscriptionChange APIs, but both are multiplexed through the same per-peer send queue and EventStream. If eventmesh-go-85 chooses true separate streams, refactor GRPCPeerLink to maintain independent data/control streams, queues, error handling, and reconnection loops. Otherwise close this as superseded and keep improving the multiplexed stream.

title: Update MeshNode integration for separate data/control plane transports
description: Modify propagateSubscriptionChange in internal/meshnode/node.go to use control plane transport. Update PublishEvent to use data plane transport only. Implement separate error handling, retry logic, and dependency injection for each transport plane.
close_reason: Implemented: MeshNode PublishEvent uses SendEvent data-plane method and subscription propagation uses SendSubscriptionChange control-plane method. Remaining retry/metrics work is tracked separately.

title: Add independent monitoring and metrics for data/control planes
description: Add separate monitoring for data-plane vs control-plane behavior. Current code has peer-level metrics, focused interfaces, and typed messages, but it does not yet expose independent throughput, latency, error, queue depth, or drop metrics by plane. This should follow the decision in eventmesh-go-85 so metrics match the final transport shape.

title: Update tests and documentation for separated data/control planes
description: Update integration tests for new separate stream architecture. Add comprehensive tests for independent failure scenarios (control plane down but data plane operational, and vice versa). Update CLAUDE.md and internal documentation to reflect new data/control plane separation patterns and Interface Segregation architecture.

title: Fix remaining linting issues
description: Address remaining 43 linting issues: 39 errcheck (unchecked Close() calls), 4 staticcheck (empty branches, unused results)

title: Health endpoint mutates EventLog state
description: internal/meshnode/node.go GetHealth appends a __health.check event to test EventLog health. Health checks should be side-effect-free; this currently inflates event statistics and pollutes the log. Replace with a non-mutating health probe or component status check.
acceptance_criteria: Calling GET /api/v1/health repeatedly does not change EventLog statistics, topic counts, offsets, or replay results.
notes: Implementation added: GetHealth now uses EventLog.GetStatistics instead of appending __health.check, with regression test TestGRPCMeshNode_GetHealthDoesNotMutateEventLog. Validation blocked locally because go/gofmt are not on PATH; see eventmesh-go-94.
close_reason: Fixed: GetHealth now uses EventLog.GetStatistics instead of appending __health.check. Added TestGRPCMeshNode_GetHealthDoesNotMutateEventLog. Focused meshnode health tests pass.

title: Publish API returns pre-append offset
description: internal/httpapi/handlers.go creates eventRecord, calls meshNode.PublishEvent, then builds the response from eventRecord.Offset. EventLog.AppendEvent returns a new event with the assigned offset, so the HTTP response/log can report offset 0 even when storage assigned a later offset. Return the persisted event metadata from MeshNode or otherwise read the assigned offset safely.
acceptance_criteria: Publishing multiple events to the same topic returns monotonically increasing offsets and event IDs matching replay/read results.
close_reason: Fixed: HTTP publish responses now use the persisted EventLog-assigned event returned by GRPCMeshNode.PublishEventWithResult. Added regression coverage for sequential offsets/event IDs matching persisted events. Full go test ./... -timeout 2m passes.

title: Align SSE topic query with persistent subscription model
description: pkg/httpclient.Stream sends GET /api/v1/events/stream?topic=..., but internal/httpapi.StreamEvents ignores the topic query and only registers the SSE client for existing MeshNode subscriptions. This makes client StreamConfig.Topic misleading unless a persistent subscription already exists. Decide the contract and update server/client/tests/docs together.
acceptance_criteria: A client using StreamConfig{Topic: "x"} receives topic x events according to documented behavior, either by creating/using a temporary stream subscription or by returning a clear error that persistent subscription creation is required.
close_reason: Aligned SSE topic handling with unified subscriptions: direct topic query is rejected with guidance, StreamConfig.Topic creates/cleans up a temporary subscription, and stream registration no longer mutates subscription metadata.

title: Make local verification boring
description: Broaden the original Makefile CLI build task into the first close-the-loop improvement: make build produce both server and CLI, document the verification loop for agents/humans, and make the local commands explicit enough for Codex, Claude, or another agent to validate their own work.
acceptance_criteria: Running make build creates both bin/eventmesh and bin/eventmesh-cli, and README build instructions match the Makefile behavior.
close_reason: Makefile builds both EventMesh binaries and agent docs now document the local verification loop.

title: Install or document local Go toolchain requirement
description: This workspace currently has no go executable on PATH, so make/test/build commands cannot run from the agent environment despite go.mod requiring Go 1.25.1. Add a repo-local toolchain note or setup script, or configure the development environment so make test can run reliably.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh contributor or agent can run go version and make test using documented setup steps.
close_reason: Fixed: installed Go via Homebrew. go version reports go1.26.2 and gofmt is available at /opt/homebrew/bin/gofmt. Codex test runs use temporary /tmp Go caches to avoid changing persistent Go env.

title: Fix cmd/eventmesh HTTP integration startup test
description: Full go test ./... with temporary Go caches failed in cmd/eventmesh: TestHTTPIntegration could not connect to http://localhost:8082/ and got connection refused. Investigate server startup timing, port configuration, or test expectations for the HTTP API.
acceptance_criteria: go test ./cmd/eventmesh passes reliably without requiring a manually-started server.
close_reason: Fixed: TestHTTPIntegration now exercises the HTTP server in-process via httptest instead of shelling out to go run and dialing fixed localhost:8082. go test ./cmd/eventmesh passes.

title: Fix PeerLink-to-MeshNode integration routing failure
description: Full go test ./... failed internal/meshnode TestPeerLinkToMeshNodeIntegration. Sender EventLog had 1 event, receiver EventLog had 0 events, sender had 1 connected peer, but getInterestedPeers found 0 interested peers for peerlink.integration.test. Investigate subscription propagation timing/state and intelligent routing expectations.
acceptance_criteria: go test ./internal/meshnode -run TestPeerLinkToMeshNodeIntegration passes reliably and verifies subscriber receives peer-routed event.
close_reason: Fixed: TestPeerLinkToMeshNodeIntegration now uses ephemeral ports, actual PeerLink listening address, and condition-based waits for inbound connection, subscription gossip, and event delivery. Focused test passes.

title: Documentation cleanup follow-up
description: Continue documentation cleanup beyond top-level Markdown: align script output, embedded help/API text, stale comments, and remaining contributor guidance with the current EventMesh implementation.
close_reason: Aligned follow-up documentation surfaces: public package docs, multi-node script output/defaults, example guidance, and agent status language; verified Markdown links, shell syntax, gofmt, and focused Go tests.

title: Deduplicate agent guidance files
description: Make AGENTS.md the canonical agent guide and reduce CLAUDE.md to a compatibility pointer so agent instructions do not drift.
close_reason: Made AGENTS.md the canonical agent guide, reduced CLAUDE.md to a Claude Code compatibility pointer, and updated docs references; Markdown links verified.

title: Shrink benchmark documentation
description: Reduce benchmark documentation to a small pointer to the existing benchmark tests and commands, avoiding premature performance claims until the implementation is more complete.
close_reason: Reduced docs/benchmark.md to a small pointer for the existing routing-table benchmarks, updated docs index wording, and verified the documented benchmark command.
